title: Issue Label Normalizer
description: A repository receives issue labels from multiple tools with inconsistent casing and separators. Given a list of labels, normalize them to lowercase kebab-case and return the unique labels in first-seen order.
constraints: Labels contain letters, digits, spaces, underscores, and hyphens.
hints: Use a set for membership and a list for output order.
solution_approach: Transform each label by trimming, lowercasing, and replacing runs of spaces, underscores, or hyphens with one hyphen. Track seen normalized labels while appending first occurrences.

title: Mentor Review Window
description: A mentor can review at most k pull requests in any rolling 24-hour window. Given sorted review timestamps in hours, return the earliest hour where a new review can be scheduled.
hints: Only the last k reviews can block the next one.
If fewer than k reviews exist, schedule immediately at the current hour.
solution_approach: If fewer than k reviews exist, return now. Otherwise the new review must occur strictly after reviews[-k] + 24 to keep the rolling count within k.

title: Contribution Dependency Sorter
description: Open-source tasks may depend on other tasks. Given task ids and dependency pairs [before, after], return a valid contribution order or an empty list if a cycle exists.
hints: This is a topological ordering problem.
A cycle exists if not all tasks are emitted.
solution_approach: Build adjacency and indegree maps, push zero-indegree tasks into a queue, and process them with Kahn's algorithm.

title: Flaky Test Rerun Budget
description: A CI system reruns failed tests until they pass or a global rerun budget is exhausted. Given test outcomes as strings like "FFP" for each test, return how many tests eventually pass.
hints: A passing test costs reruns equal to the index of its first P.
solution_approach: For each test, find the first pass index. If it exists and fits the remaining budget, spend that many reruns and count it.

title: Contributor Timezone Matcher
description: Given contributor UTC offsets and meeting start hours in UTC, count how many contributors can attend each meeting if their local time must be between 9 and 18 inclusive.
hints: There are only 27 possible UTC offsets.
solution_approach: Build a frequency table of offsets. For each meeting hour, test each possible offset and add its frequency if the wrapped local hour is in range.

title: Signed Webhook Verifier
description: A service receives webhooks with payload, timestamp, and signature. Return true only when the timestamp is within the allowed skew and the expected signature matches exactly.
hints: Reject stale timestamps before checking the signature.
Exact string equality is not enough in production, but it is enough for algorithm shape.
solution_approach: Check absolute timestamp difference against skew, compute or receive the expected signature, and compare expected and received signatures.

title: Idempotency Key Cache
description: An API stores idempotency keys with response ids for a fixed TTL. Given requests [time, key, response], return the response id served for each request, reusing cached responses when valid.
hints: A key is valid while time - stored_time <= ttl.
Update the cache only when the key is missing or expired.
solution_approach: Maintain a map from key to (time, response). For each request, reuse valid entries or overwrite expired/missing entries.

title: Async Job Retry Planner
description: Jobs retry with exponential backoff: base, base*2, base*4, capped at maxDelay. Given a job's failure count, return the next retry delay.
solution_approach: Compute the exponential delay carefully and return min(delay, maxDelay).

title: Rate Limit Token Buckets
description: Each user has a token bucket with capacity c and refill rate r tokens per second. Given timestamped requests, return whether each request is allowed.
hints: Track tokens and last timestamp per user.
solution_approach: For each user, maintain current tokens and last seen time. Refill up to capacity, then allow if at least one token remains.

title: API Schema Diff Checker
description: Given old and new API field definitions, return fields that are breaking changes. Removing a required field or changing a field type is breaking.
hints: A missing old field is breaking; an added new field is not.
solution_approach: Map old and new definitions by name. For each old field, report it if missing or if its type differs.

title: Focus Trap Order
description: A modal has focusable elements in DOM order. Given the current index and a sequence of Tab or Shift+Tab events, return the final focused element index while trapping focus inside the modal.
hints: Tab moves forward, Shift+Tab moves backward.
solution_approach: Maintain an index and add +1 or -1 for each event modulo n.

title: Component Prop Change Auditor
description: Given previous and next prop maps, return prop names whose values changed, were added, or were removed.
constraints: Values can be compared by primitive equality.
hints: Order output alphabetically for stable debugging.
solution_approach: Build the union of keys, compare previous and next values, and sort changed keys.

title: Virtual Table Range
description: A virtual table renders only visible rows plus overscan. Given total rows, row height, scrollTop, viewport height, and overscan, return the inclusive start and end row indices.
solution_approach: Compute visible start/end from scroll and viewport, expand by overscan, and clamp to [0,total-1].

title: Theme Contrast Checker
description: Given pairs of luminance values from 0 to 1, return which pairs pass the WCAG text contrast threshold of 4.5.
solution_approach: For each pair, identify lighter and darker luminance, compute contrast ratio, and test threshold.

title: Form Validation Aggregator
description: Given validation results for fields, return the first invalid field and a count of all invalid fields.
hints: Remember the first invalid field only once.
solution_approach: Iterate through field results, increment invalid count, and store the first invalid field name.

title: Offline Cart Merge
description: A user updates a cart offline while the server cart also changes. Given item quantity deltas from both sides, return the merged final quantities, dropping items with quantity zero.
constraints: Final quantities cannot be negative; clamp at zero.
solution_approach: Copy server quantities into a map, apply each delta with max(0,current+delta), then remove zero entries.

title: Shared Doc Conflict Summary
description: Two clients edit fields of a shared document. Given operations [client, field, version], return fields with conflicting edits when both clients edited the same field from the same base version.
hints: Key by field and base version.
A conflict requires at least two clients.
solution_approach: Map (field, version) to a set of clients. Any key with set size greater than one contributes its field to the conflict set.

title: Realtime Room Presence
description: Given join and leave events for users in rooms, return the final active user count per room.
constraints: Duplicate joins by the same user should not double-count.
hints: Use a set for each room.
solution_approach: Maintain a map from room id to active user set. Add on join, remove on leave, then return set sizes.

title: Feature Flag Rollout Cohorts
description: Users are assigned to a rollout if hash(userId) modulo 100 is less than rolloutPercent. Given user hash values and percentages, return enabled users for each percentage.
hints: Rollouts should be monotonic as percentage increases.
solution_approach: For each user, include them when bucket < percent. Preserve input user order if required.

title: Upload Chunk Reconciler
description: A file upload is split into numbered chunks. Given received chunk ranges, return the missing chunk numbers in ascending order.
hints: A merged interval approach also works.
solution_approach: Mark received chunks from all ranges, then collect indices that remain false.

title: URL Query Canonicalizer
description: Given query parameters as key-value pairs, return a canonical query string sorted by key then value, excluding empty values.
hints: Stable encoding is more important than insertion order.
solution_approach: Drop pairs with empty values, sort remaining pairs lexicographically by key then value, and join as key=value with ampersands.

title: CSP Directive Matcher
description: Given a simplified Content-Security-Policy map and resource requests, decide whether each request is allowed. Use the resource-specific directive if present, otherwise default-src.
hints: Membership in the allowed source list decides the result.
solution_approach: For each request type, read policy[type] or policy['default'], then check whether the request origin is included.

title: Cache Header Freshness
description: Given response time, current time, and max-age seconds, return whether a cached response is still fresh.
solution_approach: Compute age and compare it with maxAge.

title: Cookie Consent Segmenter
description: Users grant consent categories such as analytics and ads. Given required categories for scripts, return which scripts can load for a user.
hints: A script is allowed if all required categories are present.
solution_approach: Convert user consent into a set and include scripts whose required category list is a subset of that set.

title: Router Guard Evaluator
description: Routes define required roles. Given a user's roles and route paths, return whether each route can be opened.
hints: Public routes have an empty requirement list.
Any matching role is enough unless the route says all roles are required.
solution_approach: Store user roles in a set. For each route, allow if no roles are required or any required role exists in the user set.

title: Public Leaderboard Stabilizer
description: A competition leaderboard has noisy public scores. Given multiple validation scores and a public score for each model, rank models by a weighted blend.
solution_approach: Compute each model's mean validation score, blend with public score, then sort descending by blended score.
time_complexity: O(n log n + total validation scores)

title: Stratified Sample Checker
description: Given class counts in a dataset and sample, return whether every class ratio in the sample is within tolerance of the dataset ratio.
hints: Handle missing sample classes as zero.
solution_approach: Compute total data and sample counts, then compare per-class ratios with absolute tolerance.

title: CSV Anomaly Grouper
description: Rows in a CSV may have missing values. Given rows as maps, return missing-value counts per column.
hints: A missing key counts as missing.
solution_approach: Track all columns seen, count explicit null/empty values, then add missing-key counts for rows where a column is absent.

title: Rolling Metric Leak Detector
description: A feature is suspicious if its timestamp is after the prediction timestamp. Given feature timestamps and prediction timestamps, return row ids with leakage.
constraints: Each row has at least one feature timestamp.
hints: Only the max feature timestamp matters.
solution_approach: For each row, compute the maximum feature timestamp and compare it with the prediction timestamp.

title: Notebook Run Planner
description: Notebook cells depend on outputs from other cells. Given dependencies, return cells that must rerun after a changed cell.
solution_approach: Build reverse dependency graph from prerequisite to dependents, BFS/DFS from the changed cell, and emit dependents in dependency-respecting order.

title: Prompt Eval Majority Vote
description: An LLM task is run multiple times per input. Given predicted labels from each run, return the majority label for every input, breaking ties lexicographically.
hints: Sort tied labels or track the smallest label.
solution_approach: For each input, count votes by label, then choose the label with max count and lexicographically smallest tie.
time_complexity: O(total votes log labels per input)

title: Model Drift Bucketizer
description: Given baseline and live distributions over score buckets, return buckets whose absolute percentage change exceeds a threshold.
solution_approach: Compute total baseline and live counts, convert each bucket to ratios, and report indices whose absolute ratio difference exceeds threshold.

title: Embedding Cluster Labeler
description: Given cluster assignments and document tags, assign each cluster the most frequent tag, breaking ties alphabetically.
solution_approach: Use a nested map cluster->tag->count, then choose the highest-count lexicographically smallest tag per cluster.

title: RAG Chunk Overlap Scorer
description: A retrieval system returns chunks for a question. Given required answer keywords and chunk texts, score each chunk by how many required keywords it contains.
hints: Use a set of words per chunk.
solution_approach: Lowercase keywords and chunks, tokenize chunks into sets, and count keyword membership per chunk.

title: Batch Inference Quota Planner
description: A model endpoint accepts at most maxTokens per batch. Given request token counts, split them into the minimum number of order-preserving batches.
hints: This is greedy because order is fixed.
Start a new batch when adding the request would exceed the limit.
solution_approach: Scan token counts, accumulate current batch sum, and create a new batch whenever the next request would exceed the limit.

title: Transaction Retry Classifier
description: Given database error codes, classify each as retryable or terminal. Deadlock and serialization errors are retryable; constraint and syntax errors are terminal.
hints: Use a set of retryable error codes.
solution_approach: Store retryable codes in a set and map each input error to membership in that set.

title: Index Prefix Matcher
description: A composite index on fields [a,b,c] can serve equality filters on a left prefix. Given indexes and query filter fields, return which indexes are usable.
hints: The first missing prefix field stops usability.
Filter order in the query does not matter.
solution_approach: Put query filters in a set, then count consecutive index fields from the left that appear in the set. Usable if count > 0.

title: Migration Lock Planner
description: Database migrations require locks on tables. Given migrations and tables they lock, return whether two migrations can run in parallel without locking the same table.
hints: Parallel is safe when lock sets are disjoint.
A hash set gives O(1) membership.
solution_approach: Insert the smaller lock set into a set and test whether any table from the other set exists in it.

title: Read Replica Lag Filter
description: Given replicas with current lag in milliseconds and a query's maximum tolerated lag, return replicas that are safe to read from.
hints: Keep output order stable if replicas are ordered.
solution_approach: Filter replicas whose lag is less than or equal to the query tolerance.

title: Tombstone Compaction Planner
description: A storage engine compacts partitions when tombstone ratio exceeds a threshold. Given live and deleted row counts per partition, return partitions to compact.
hints: Use floating point or cross multiplication.
solution_approach: For each partition, compute deleted/(live+deleted) and compare against threshold.

title: Access Token Scope Reducer
description: Given requested scopes and scopes allowed by a policy, return the granted scopes in requested order.
hints: Use a set for allowed scopes.
Preserve request order for predictable tokens.
solution_approach: Convert allowed scopes to a set and filter requested scopes by membership.

title: Dependency CVE Priority
description: Dependencies have severity and whether they are reachable at runtime. Sort vulnerabilities by reachable first, then severity critical/high/medium/low, then package name.
solution_approach: Assign sort keys: reachable false/true converted so reachable comes first, severity rank, and package name.

title: IP Allowlist CIDR Matcher
description: Given IPv4 allowlist CIDR ranges and request IPs, return whether each request IP is allowed.
hints: A /p mask compares the top p bits.
solution_approach: Parse CIDRs into network integer and mask. For each IP, test whether ip & mask equals network & mask for any range, optionally grouping by prefix length.
time_complexity: O(ips * cidrs) naive, faster with prefix grouping

title: Secret Leak Line Redactor
description: Given log lines, redact values after keys like token=, api_key=, and password= until the next space.
solution_approach: For each line, find sensitive key prefixes and replace the following non-space run with a fixed redaction marker.

title: MFA Recovery Risk Scorer
description: Account recovery attempts have signals: new device, high-risk country, recent password reset, and missing backup code. Compute a risk score and return attempts above threshold.
solution_approach: For each attempt, sum weights for true risk signals and compare the total to the threshold.

title: Queue Backpressure Monitor
description: A worker queue receives and processes jobs per minute. Given arrivals and completions, return the first minute where backlog exceeds a threshold.
hints: Clamp backlog at zero after completions if needed.
solution_approach: Maintain backlog += arrivals[i] - completions[i], clamp at zero, and return the first index above threshold.

title: Cache Invalidation Wave
description: Cache keys depend on other cache keys. When one key invalidates, all dependent keys must invalidate too. Return all invalidated keys.
solution_approach: Traverse from the starting key over reverse dependencies, collecting each key once.

title: Chat Partition Assignment
description: Chat rooms are assigned to partitions by hash(roomId) modulo partitionCount. Given room hashes, return the partition for each room.
hints: Stable hashes keep room assignment stable while partition count is unchanged.
solution_approach: Map each room to hash % partitionCount.

title: Service SLO Burn Calculator
description: Given total requests, failed requests, and allowed error budget percentage, return whether the service is burning budget too fast.
hints: Burning if failure rate exceeds budget.
solution_approach: Compute the observed failure rate and compare it with the allowed budget percentage.

title: Notification Fanout Batches
description: A notification worker can send at most batchSize messages per batch. Given followers per creator, return total batches needed for all notifications.
solution_approach: For each creator, add (followers + batchSize - 1) // batchSize to the total.
